items.text: How to apply for a TAFE course

title: How to Apply for a TAFE Course
introduction: This page explains how to choose a TAFE course, apply and enrol.
openingParagraphs: TAFE has many courses that can help you learn skills for work and life.
Applying for a course can involve several choices and steps.
You can ask someone you trust to read this information with you.

sections.title: Choose a course
introductionParagraphs: Start by finding a course that matches what you want to learn.

blocks.title: Look at your course choices

paragraphs.text: TAFE offers courses in many different subjects.
Think about the skills you want to learn and the work you might want to do.

blocks.imgAlt: A student comparing three different course choices

blocks.title: Talk with TAFE about your choices

paragraphs.text: A TAFE support person can explain courses and help you compare them.
You can talk with someone in person or over the phone.

blocks.imgAlt: A TAFE support person discussing course choices with a student

blocks.title: Ask for communication support

paragraphs.text: Tell TAFE if you need information in another language or format.
You can also ask a friend, family member or support person to help you.

blocks.imgAlt: Hands sharing a picture booklet and headphones for communication support

sections.title: Before you apply
introductionParagraphs: Get the information you need before you start your application.

blocks.title: Find your Unique Student Identifier

paragraphs.text: A Unique Student Identifier is also called a USI.
A USI is a number that identifies you when you study at TAFE.
You may already have a USI if you studied before.

blocks.imgAlt: A student checking identification information on a phone

blocks.title: Choose where you will study

paragraphs.text: Some courses are taught at a TAFE campus.
Some courses let you study online from home.
Check which choices are available for your course.

blocks.imgAlt: A student choosing between studying at a campus or online

blocks.title: Check how much the course costs

paragraphs.text: Look at the course page to find out how much you may need to pay.
Some people can pay a lower fee or may not need to pay a fee.
Ask TAFE to explain the fees if you are unsure.

blocks.imgAlt: A student checking a course fee and a possible discount

blocks.title: Get your documents ready

paragraphs.text: Some courses ask you to prove your identity or show what you studied before.
Keep the documents together so they are ready when you apply.

blocks.imgAlt: A student putting identification and a certificate into a folder

sections.title: Understand the course status
introductionParagraphs: The course page may show different options depending on whether places are available.

blocks.title: Enrolment open

paragraphs.text: Enrolment open means you can sign up for the course now.
You will need to follow the enrolment steps and arrange payment.

blocks.imgAlt: A hand selecting an open enrolment option on a tablet

blocks.title: Application required

paragraphs.text: Application required means TAFE needs to check that the course is right for you.
You may need to show that you meet the course entry requirements.

blocks.imgAlt: A magnifying glass checking identity and course requirement documents

blocks.title: Register your interest

paragraphs.text: Register your interest means you tell TAFE that you want to study the course.
TAFE may contact you to talk about the course before you can apply.

blocks.imgAlt: A hand registering interest in a course on a phone

blocks.title: Waitlist open

paragraphs.text: Waitlist open means the course is full at the moment.
You can join the waiting list.
TAFE will contact you if a place becomes available.

blocks.imgAlt: A hand holding a waiting ticket beside a clock and queue

sections.title: Apply and enrol
introductionParagraphs: Follow the instructions for the option shown on your course page.

blocks.title: Complete the application

paragraphs.text: Answer each question in the application.
Add any documents that TAFE asks for.
Check your answers before you submit the application.

blocks.imgAlt: A student completing an online application on a laptop

blocks.title: Wait for TAFE to contact you

paragraphs.text: TAFE may send you an email, call you or send you a message.
Check your messages so you do not miss important information.

blocks.imgAlt: A student receiving a message after waiting for a response

blocks.title: Accept your place and enrol

paragraphs.text: TAFE will tell you if you can join the course.
Follow the instructions to accept your place, enrol and arrange payment.
Ask TAFE for help if you do not understand the instructions.

blocks.imgAlt: Hands accepting a TAFE course offer

sections.title: Students from overseas
introductionParagraphs: Different application rules may apply if you are not an Australian citizen or permanent resident.

blocks.title: Check how international students apply

paragraphs.text: International students may need to use a different application process.
Check the information for international students before you apply.

blocks.imgAlt: An international student checking study requirements for Australia

blocks.title: Check the rules for your visa

paragraphs.text: A visa is a document that gives you permission to be in Australia for a period of time.
Your visa may affect which courses you can study and how you apply.
Ask for advice if you are not sure which rules apply to you.

blocks.imgAlt: A passport and visa requirements being checked for study in Australia

about.aboutDescription: This page uses Easy Read. Easy Read uses simple words, clear structure and meaningful pictures to make information easier to understand.
